chunk_summary: This section focuses on basic responses to foreign body airway obstruction in infants and children during complementary feeding, referencing 2025 AHA guidelines and algorithms for pediatric basic life support (BLS).
learning_objectives: Understand the steps for managing foreign body airway obstruction in infants and children.
Learn the 2025 AHA pediatric BLS algorithms for airway obstruction response.
Recognize choking hazards during complementary feeding for ages 6–24 months.
Apply evidence-based first aid techniques to prevent choking-related complications.
key_facts: Foreign body airway obstruction is a leading cause of preventable injury in children under 24 months.
The 2025 AHA guidelines emphasize age-specific algorithms for infants and children.
Infant BLS algorithm includes back slaps and chest thrusts for airway obstruction management.
Child BLS algorithm recommends abdominal thrusts for children over 1 year.
Immediate intervention can significantly reduce mortality rates in choking incidents.
Complementary feeding introduces choking risks, requiring vigilant food preparation and supervision.
reference_quote: Part 6: Pediatric basic life support — 2025 American Heart Association and American Academy of Pediatrics guidelines — Algorithm: BLS infant foreign-body airway obstruction.
document_key_topics: Choking hazard prevention during complementary feeding
Basic life support for infants and children
